channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Teenagers sue Musk's xAI claiming image-generator made sexually explicit images of them as minors
item.description: Three teenagers in Tennessee have sued Elon Musk’s xAI, claiming the company’s image-generation tools were used to morph real photos of them into explicitly sexual images.
item.dc:creator: Travis Loller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 3 men are charged with conspiring to smuggle US artificial intelligence to China
item.description: A senior vice president of Super Micro Computer Inc. and two others affiliated with the company have been charged with conspiring to smuggle billions of dollars of computer servers containing advanced Nvidia chips to China.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: CISA urges IT to harden endpoint management systems after cyberattack by pro-Iranian group
item.description: The US is urging infosec leaders to harden their endpoint management system configurations after last week’s hack of American medical supplies provider Stryker by pro-Iranian threat actor Handala.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meteorite hunters scour Ohio for fragments of 7-ton space rock that crashed into Earth
item.description: Meteorite hunters are combing Ohio for fragments of a space rock that streaked across the sky earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Aftoora-Orsagos And Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: IDC: Dell leads server market driven by AI infrastructure needs
item.description: Despite tariffs and parts shortages, the server market reached a record $125.3 billion dollars in revenue during the fourth quarter of 2025, driven by the accelerated investment in AI Infrastructure, according to the International Data Corporation’s (IDC) Worldwide Quarterly Server Tracker.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The companies that win with AI may not look like companies at all
item.description: For the past two years, the dominant corporate conversation around artificial intelligence has been painfully predictable. Executives talk about productivity, copilots, efficiency gains, and cost savings. Boards demand AI road maps. Consultants package urgency into slides.
item.dc:creator: Enrique Dans, Fast Company

item.title: 9 reasons Java is still great
item.description: In a world obsessed with disruption, Java threads the needle between stability and innovation. It’s the ultimate syncretic platform, synthesizing the best ideas from functional programming, concurrency, cloud computing, and AI under a reliable, battle-tested umbrella.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI’s new frontier models mark a huge change in how AI will be built
item.description: In early March, OpenAI unleashed a one-two punch, dropping two major frontier models just days apart. First, we got the new GPT-5.3, an “instant” model optimized for fast, accurate responses. Then, OpenAI released GPT-5.4 two days later. This is a “thinking” model optimized for deep analytical work.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Smith, Fast Company

item.title: How surveillance tech led police to accuse the wrong person
item.description: Police dropped charges against a Colorado woman after phone data and video disproved evidence suggesting she was involved in a porch theft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reskilling wont save us from AI. Here’s what we need to do instead
item.description: Nearly every major policy paper, and the wannabe thought leaders that quote them, says that university enrollment and programming skills are the winning combination for the next Industrial Revolution. My analysis of 11 million professional programmers at Gild completely disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Vivienne Ming, Fast Company

item.title: AI could be suppressing wages for young workers
item.description: Growing AI adoption has slowed the hiring of young workers in software development and customer service jobs, and recent data suggests it could also be exerting pressure on entry-level wages, according industry experts.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Genealogy boom exposes personal data scammers can exploit
item.description: Using genealogy websites can expose personal data that scammers use to target families, according to experts warning about privacy risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Snowflake’s new ‘autonomous’ AI layer aims to do the work, not just answer questions
item.description: Snowflake has taken the covers off a product, currently under development, which it describes as an “autonomous” AI layer that promises to turn its data cloud from a place that answers questions about data into one that actually does the work: stitching together analysis, reports, and even slide dec
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 6 ways Gemini supercharges Google Sheets
item.description: Google’s AI assistant, Gemini, can assist you in several ways in Google Sheets, from analyzing your data to helping you edit your spreadsheet faster. In Google Sheets, you mostly use these genAI tools through the Gemini sidebar.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Beyond the fan: Crossing the liquid cooling rubicon
item.description: The infrastructure inflection point The artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure revolution has made an unlikely discipline suddenly relevant: thermodynamics.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia targets inference as AI’s next battleground with Groq 3 LPX
item.description: 2026 is predicted to be the year that AI moves from pilot to production, becoming measurably useful across the enterprise. But while many businesses are ready, the underlying infrastructure doesn’t seem to be, particularly when it comes to next-stage inferencing.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Robot firefighters enter burning buildings first
item.description: New robotic firefighting vehicles use thermal cameras and water cannons to locate fires and hazards before firefighters step inside a building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CarGurus breach linked to ShinyHunters exposes 12.4M records
item.description: CarGurus users face a security risk after the ShinyHunters hacking group allegedly leaked 12.4 million records containing names, addresses and finance data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
